title: Teen Open Studio
short_description: Just for teens, this open studio offers drop-in art making inspired by a different exhibition each week, all summer long.
description:
Just for teens, this open studio takes place on selected Fridays and Saturdays this summer and offers art-making activities inspired by a different exhibition each week. Join us to express yourself through a variety of media and techniques. All materials will be provided, and no experience is necessary.

Drop in anytime between 11:00 and 3:00 on the following Fridays and Saturdays:

June 28 and 29
Found-Object Photograms
Inspired by János Megyik Photograms

July 12 and 13
Expressive Printmaking
Inspired by A Sign of Things to Come: Prints by Japanese Women Artists after 1950

July 19 and 20
Stitching Identity
Inspired by Threaded Visions: Contemporary Weaving from the Collection

July 26 and 27
Painting Personal Landscapes
Inspired by Georgia O’Keeffe: “My New Yorks”

August 9 and 10
Chance Collage
Inspired by Ellsworth Kelly: Spectrum Colors Arranged by Chance

Looking for a deeper dive? Join us for Teen Studio Workshops, offered 1:00 to 4:00 on selected Thursdays. These guided art-making sessions feature a visit to the galleries to explore the related exhibition. Registration for the first Thursday session, Found-Object Photograms, is currently open now. Check back soon to register for additional events in this series.
